what_it_does: Defines a shared, token-driven CSS surface for product chrome and screen archetypes: phone mockup shells, standalone 390×844 screens, immersive unit views, decision cards, a generic archetype renderer, and presentation/explained-view layouts. It maps design-system tokens and semantic color variables to concrete component classes so application screens can be composed rather than redrawn.
observations: A single large stylesheet mixing phone chrome (.phone, .sbar, .p-hd, .p-act), standalone screen root (.screen), immersive unit view (.screen.uv), decision card (.screen.decision-card, .dc-*), generic archetype renderer (.ar-*), and two presentation modes (.ex-*, .dv-*)
Heavy use of CSS custom properties: --dna-space-*, --dna-type-*, --dna-radius-*, and semantic color vars such as --line, --cool, --warm, --gold, --ochre, --charcoal, --page, --nested

Extensive design-rationale comments that cite external files and concepts: dna/application.json, modes.json, dna/voice.json, dna/motion.json, a DECISION-CARD-HOST-CONTRACT.md, DNA.renderArchetype/renderUnit/renderPiece/renderDecision/renderExplained
Responsive layout for .screen.ar via @media breakpoints at 760px (landscape 2-col) and 1180px (desktop generous)
Decision-card options are styled as tappable buttons with a persistent ::after 'Choose ›' chevron, .recommended gold treatment, and .decided-opt state

Generic archetype (.ar-*) includes tile options, ledger-row options for 3+ choices with an ordinal metal ramp (.r-gold, .r-ochre, .r-bronze, .r-faint), a decided-moment banner, empty/failed state screens, and a 'Not now' ghost
Unit view (.uv-*) uses warm radial gradients, frosted cards, glass chips, and a corner V-hub (.uv-vee) that fans circular verb sockets on open
Presentation views (.ex-view, .dv-view) embed a live .screen.ar mockup beside or above slide-like explanatory content
File is truncated near the end of the .dv-title rule
standouts: The .ar-* generic archetype renderer is treated as a unifying 'one renderer every surface unifies to' with tile, ledger-row, decided, error, empty, and failed states
The .uv-vee corner hub and fanned socket menu is a distinct radial-verb interaction pattern with CSS-driven staggered transitions
Decision options use an explicit pseudo-element action label ('Choose ›') and a recommended gold ring to signal the one recommended choice
The stylesheet attempts to bridge design specs directly into code through long, intent-laden comments and token naming
conventions: Class prefixes group by concern: .p-* product chrome, .uv-* unit view, .dc-* decision card, .ar-* archetype, .ex-* explained presentation, .dv-* composed decision view
Sizing and spacing are almost entirely token-driven via --dna-* variables
Typography pairing: 'Plus Jakarta Sans' for UI prose, 'IBM Plex Mono' for data and labels
Decorative patterns (hatch, band, pinstripes) are implemented with pseudo-elements and repeating-linear-gradient
States are expressed by modifier classes (.recommended, .chosen, .decided-opt, .rec, .open, .write-error, .show) rather than attribute selectors
Phone chrome uses absolute positioning; standalone screens use a fixed 390×844 canvas
concerns: The file is monolithic and mixes many distinct surface concerns (phone chrome, app screens, decision cards, presentation slides) in one sheet
Many custom properties are consumed but not defined in this file, so the file cannot be understood or tested in isolation
Some comments reference external contracts and JSON files whose contents are not shown, creating implicit dependencies
The .dv-view section is described as resolving via @container queries, but no @container rules are visible in the truncated portion
Accessibility is not explicitly addressed beyond a reduced-motion guard on the streaming cursor; contrast of gold-on-warm and faint text is assumed but not verified
A large number of absolute pixel values and magic numbers are scattered throughout, increasing maintenance risk
notes: A dense, design-narrative CSS sheet that turns a warm, token-based design system into concrete phone and screen archetypes, with a strong focus on decision-card affordances and presentation embedding.
questions: Where are the --dna-* and semantic color variables (e.g., --gold, --ochre) defined?
Which JavaScript renderers consume each class family (.ar-*, .dc-*, .uv-*, .ex-*, .dv-*)?
How are the .uv-soc socket offsets (--edx, --edy) and stagger index (--i) supplied at runtime?
Is the @container-based responsive behavior for .dv-view implemented in another file or missing?
What is the exact structure of dna/application.json and the decision-card host contract referenced in comments?
Are the ConceptV line-icons referenced for .uv-soc provided as SVG symbols or an icon font?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide shared product chrome components for app pieces. The file does that, but also expands into standalone screen archetypes, immersive unit views, decision cards, generic archetype rendering, and presentation views—broader than pure phone chrome.
apparent_themes: Component reuse over bespoke per-screen CSS
Warm, immersive radial backgrounds and frosted glass accents
Signal economy: gold marks the recommended/focal action
Calm, intentional spacing and readable prose over dense dashboards
Archetype unification: one renderer for many decision contexts
Presentation-as-code: live interface embedded in explanatory slides
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to encode a complete visual grammar for a product surface so that screens, decisions, and presentations are assembled from a shared class vocabulary rather than hand-coded, keeping design intent and token semantics in one place.
novelty: The combination of a generic decision archetype renderer with tile and ledger-row variants, an ordinal metal ramp, a radial V-hub socket menu for unit verbs, and embedded live-interface presentation views in a single CSS surface.
contribution: A comprehensive, token-driven CSS vocabulary for product chrome, decision-screen archetypes, and presentation scaffolding that a fused system could keep as a reusable styling layer for consistent warm-toned surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: phone.css is a large token-driven stylesheet in counterpart-design that defines shared phone chrome, standalone 390×844 screen components, immersive unit views, decision cards, a generic archetype renderer, and presentation/explained-view layouts for a warm design system.
intention_for_embedding: To provide one reusable CSS surface that materializes the design system's application face and decision-screen archetypes across phone mockups, standalone screens, and slide presentations.
